rulings.Case Title: UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON, A LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 28, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON'S Special MOTION To Strike Pursuant To CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16: The matter is continued to December 4, 2007 for hearing The parties are to address the following issues by Friday at 4:00 p.m. with courtesy copies to Department 302: 1.Identify the allegations in the complaint that support/do not support the proposition that the lawsuit is aimed at protected activity as contrasted with a breach of fiducicairy duty. 2. Does defendant dispute the allegations of paragraph 14 of the complaint? Explain. 3. What is an "informal committee of asbestos creditors" as alleged in paragraph 16 of the complaint? 4. Explain why/why not the allegations of paragraphs 21, 26 and 27 of the complaint do not charge a breach of fiduciary duty? 5. Explain why/why not representing the "the informal" committee in a possible bankruptcy case is ripe for resolution? 6. It appears that defendant's reading of Peregrine Funding would subject any complaint to the anti-Slapp statute that asserts that the law firm is breaching its fiduciary duties by undertaking to represent an alleged adverse party in litigation. If this is not so, explain. 7. If defendant establishes the existence of an ethical wall, will that defeat plaintiff's claim? Why/why not? 8. What weight is to be given to the Goodman declaration? Why is/is not it persuasive? 9. Explain why/why not the representation of Brayton is/is not contrary to plaintiff's interests. 10. Is it defendant's position that after the discovery motion is decided in Plant that it will cease to represent Brayton/Kazan or the "informal committee" in that case or any other case? 11. The parties are to file any opposition to the evidentiary objections. This supplemental submission is not to exceed 12 pages. =(302/PJM/AY)
